snippet.title: $500M Gone: The Gardner Heist
snippet.description:
In 1990, two men dressed as police officers entered Boston's Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum. Thirteen works disappeared. More than thirty years later, the case is still unsolved and nothing has been recovered.

This Prime Documentary follows the night of the theft, the empty frames, the estimated $500 million figure, the hunt, and the mystery that still refuses to close.

Important wording: the $500 million figure is an estimate. The FBI has not publicly named the thieves. This video does not accuse any living person of the theft.

Visuals are AI-generated symbolic reconstructions, project-generated stills, licensed factory atmosphere, and motion design. They do not use real-person likenesses, the real museum interior/logo, or authentic artwork reproductions.

Chapters:
00:00 Cold open: the empty frames
00:08 Prime Documentary opening
00:12 The night
06:40 The treasure
12:40 The hunt
20:15 The mystery
26:52 Ending

Independent educational documentary. This is not legal advice.

Subscribe for documentaries about the hidden systems behind real events.
